meaning: n.全体人员,(工作)队 vbl.crow的过去式
examples: In the distance the crew sighted land.
Many crew members were up before reveille.
He is acting as liaison with the film crew.
Catching sharks is never a routine business, even for experts like Burkholder and his crew.
Not so lucky were the shipwrecked crew of an unknown vessel washed up in 1860.
